# Product Brief

## Overview

Describe the opportunity in plain language.

## Problem

What user problem, business problem, or trust problem exists today?

## Not A PRD Yet

State what remains intentionally unresolved at the requirements level so this brief does not get mistaken for a PRD.

## Why Now

Explain why this matters now for Gama's product direction, market timing, or user trust.

## Target Users

Who is this for first? Note any differences for single users, shared-household users, spreadsheet fallback users, or future FIRE-oriented users.

## Strategic Fit

Explain how this supports the decision-first thesis, admin-work elimination, Safe-to-Spend, forward-looking clarity, Crisis Cushion, privacy-first resilience, or event and narrative intelligence.

## Success Signals

List the clearest qualitative or quantitative signs that this brief is worth pursuing.

## Constraints

Document important security, privacy, lane, release, or architecture constraints.

## Open Decisions

Record unresolved issues with recommendation, rationale, and what would change the decision.
